Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails (via search_emails) for Keiko Tanaka, Mosaic Robotics, Greg Pallone, Swift Relocations, placarding, and DOT to find correspondence about the signed placarding certificate and any PDF or delivery confirmations.
OE 2: Search Slack (via conversations_search_messages) for Keiko Tanaka, placarding, Mosaic, DOT, or Swift placarding; and/or list Linear issues (via linear_list_issues with a query such as Tanaka, Mosaic, DOT, placarding, or Swift) for operations tickets about Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Class 3B laser DOT documentation or Swift placarding certificate follow-up, to see whether the certificate was received or logged internally.
OE 3: Search Slack (via conversations_search_messages) for Emeka Diallo around April 5, 2026 with terms like overload, capacity, coordinator, Mosaic, Sunbelt, Palmetto, or Axiom; and/or list Linear issues (via linear_list_issues with a query such as Emeka, rebalance, coverage, Sunbelt, Palmetto, Axiom, or Mosaic) for the April coordinator rebalance / account coverage issue tied to Emeka and those accounts.
OE 4: Map coordinator spare capacity using airtable_search_records on relocation or move-tracking tables (coordinator, status, client or account fields as available) together with CRM reads such as crm_search_deals, crm_get_deal, and/or crm_list_engagements on Mosaic and other active deals to compare coordinator assignments (e.g. Blessing Okafor, Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Javier Morales, Suki Patel) and determine whether any coordinator can absorb work from Emeka or whether coverage remains full.
OE 5: Search emails (via search_emails) for Dr. Mehta, Dr. Anand Mehta, Canopy Health, housing, UrbanNest, River North, Streeterville, chain-of-custody, equipment, or executive relocation to find status updates on housing options and chain-of-custody documentation.
OE 6: Search Slack (via conversations_search_messages) for messages from or mentioning Fatimah Al-Rashidi together with Dr. Mehta, Anand Mehta, Canopy, housing, equipment, or chain-of-custody to find the latest updates on the executive move.
OE 7: List or fetch Linear issues (via linear_list_issues with query Mehta, Canopy, or executive relocation, and/or linear_get_issue once issue IDs are known) for Dr. Anand Mehta / Canopy workstreams- including issues numbered 343, 375, and 396 (temporary housing, equipment chain-of-custody, household move) - to verify task status and recent activity; use linear_list_comments on those issues if comment history matters for status.
OE 8: If no recent detailed update on Dr. Mehta's workstreams is found, send a Slack message (via conversations_add_message) to Fatimah Al-Rashidi requesting a status report on all workstreams. First resolve the correct Slack channel or DM (e.g. via conversations_search_messages, conversations_history, or contacts_search_contacts for Fatimah / fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com) so the message is delivered to the right conversation.
OE 9: If no clean reassignment or available coordinator is found for Emeka, schedule a 15-minute check-in (via calendar_add_calendar_event) with Emeka Diallo (use emeka.diallo@moveops.com or resolve via contacts_search_contacts) to discuss shifting coverage or support, with duration and title reflecting a short coordination meeting.
OE 10: If the signed DOT placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka is still not on file after email and ticket review, send follow-up email (via send_email or reply_to_email) to Greg Pallone at greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com (or use the active Chloe–Greg thread) about the missing certificate and regulatory urgency.
OE 11: Log findings and updates in CRM (via crm_create_engagement on the relevant company or deal, e.g. Mosaic Robotics, Canopy Health, or related deals) and/or on Linear (via linear_create_comment on the relevant DOT or Mehta issues, or via linear_update_issue to append investigation notes to an existing relevant issue) so the audit trail reflects cert status, coordinator capacity conclusions, and Mehta move status.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails (via search_emails) for Keiko Tanaka, Mosaic Robotics, Greg Pallone, Swift Relocations, placarding, and DOT to find correspondence about the signed placarding certificate and any PDF or delivery confirmations.
OE 2: Search Slack (via conversations_search_messages) for Keiko Tanaka, placarding, Mosaic, DOT, or Swift placarding; and/or list Linear issues (via linear_list_issues with a query such as Tanaka, Mosaic, DOT, placarding, or Swift) for operations tickets about Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Class 3B laser DOT documentation or Swift placarding certificate follow-up, to see whether the certificate was received or logged internally.
OE 3: Search Slack (via conversations_search_messages) for Emeka Diallo around April 5, 2026 with terms like overload, capacity, coordinator, Mosaic, Sunbelt, Palmetto, or Axiom; and/or list Linear issues (via linear_list_issues with a query such as Emeka, rebalance, coverage, Sunbelt, Palmetto, Axiom, or Mosaic) for the April coordinator rebalance / account coverage issue tied to Emeka and those accounts.
OE 4: Map coordinator spare capacity using airtable_search_records on relocation or move-tracking tables (coordinator, status, client or account fields as available) together with CRM reads such as crm_search_deals, crm_get_deal, and/or crm_list_engagements on Mosaic and other active deals to compare coordinator assignments (e.g. Blessing Okafor, Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Javier Morales, Suki Patel) and determine whether any coordinator can absorb work from Emeka or whether coverage remains full.
OE 5: Search emails (via search_emails) for Dr. Mehta, Dr. Anand Mehta, Canopy Health, housing, UrbanNest, River North, Streeterville, chain-of-custody, equipment, or executive relocation to find status updates on housing options and chain-of-custody documentation.
OE 6: Search Slack (via conversations_search_messages) for messages from or mentioning Fatimah Al-Rashidi together with Dr. Mehta, Anand Mehta, Canopy, housing, equipment, or chain-of-custody to find the latest updates on the executive move.
OE 7: List or fetch Linear issues (via linear_list_issues with query Mehta, Canopy, or executive relocation, and/or linear_get_issue once issue IDs are known) for Dr. Anand Mehta / Canopy workstreams- including issues numbered 343, 375, and 396 (temporary housing, equipment chain-of-custody, household move) - to verify task status and recent activity; use linear_list_comments on those issues if comment history matters for status.
OE 8: If no recent detailed update on Dr. Mehta's workstreams is found, send a Slack message (via conversations_add_message) to Fatimah Al-Rashidi requesting a status report on all workstreams. First resolve the correct Slack channel or DM (e.g. via conversations_search_messages, conversations_history, or contacts_search_contacts for Fatimah / fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com) so the message is delivered to the right conversation.
OE 9: If no clean reassignment or available coordinator is found for Emeka, schedule a 15-minute check-in (via calendar_add_calendar_event) with Emeka Diallo (use emeka.diallo@moveops.com or resolve via contacts_search_contacts) to discuss shifting coverage or support, with duration and title reflecting a short coordination meeting.
OE 10: If the signed DOT placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka is still not on file after email and ticket review, send follow-up email (via send_email or reply_to_email) to Greg Pallone at greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com (or use the active Chloe–Greg thread) about the missing certificate and regulatory urgency.
OE 11: Log findings and updates in CRM (via crm_create_engagement on the relevant company or deal, e.g. Mosaic Robotics, Canopy Health, or related deals) and/or on Linear (via linear_create_comment on the relevant DOT or Mehta issues, or via linear_update_issue to append investigation notes to an existing relevant issue) so the audit trail reflects cert status, coordinator capacity conclusions, and Mehta move status.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The final response summarizes whether the signed DOT placarding certificate or equivalent Swift documentation for the Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic Robotics) shipment is received, pending from Swift or Greg Pallone, or still missing in MoveOps files, with enough detail for Chloe to decide on follow-up.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response clearly states the cert is MISSING: Greg Pallone promised scanned PDFs on ~April 22 but no documents arrived in 4 days, his email had no attachments, and no follow-up cert email exists. This gives Chloe a clear status to decide on follow-up.
✓Run #2The response clearly states Greg replied on April 22 confirming placarding was completed by driver Mike Becerra (Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538), promised PDFs within 24 hours, but as of April 26 the signed PDF documents have NOT been received. It explicitly notes Greg's email had no attachments, giving Chloe clear basis for follow-up.
✓Run #3The response clearly states that Greg Pallone promised scanned PDFs within 24 hours (after April 22 reply) but as of April 26, NO documents have been received — his reply email contained zero attachments. The cert is definitively missing, which gives Chloe enough detail to decide on follow-up.
✓Run #4The response clearly states Greg Pallone replied on April 22 confirming placarding was completed (Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538), committed to sending scanned PDFs within 24 hours, but as of April 26 the actual signed documents have NOT arrived — no attachments and no follow-up email found. This gives Chloe a clear status to decide on follow-up.
✓Run #5The final response clearly states: Greg confirmed placarding was completed on April 15 and promised scanned PDFs, but 'it is now April 26 — four days later — and no scanned documents have been received. The certificate is confirmed to exist in Swift's archive but is NOT yet in MoveOps' hands.' This gives Chloe a clear status for follow-up decisions.
✓Run #6The final response clearly states that Greg Pallone replied confirming the cert exists and promised PDFs within 24 hours, but 'the actual PDF documents have NOT arrived' and 'His email contained no attachments — only a promise.' This gives Chloe a clear status to decide follow-up.
2 The final response summarizes coordinator capacity and coverage for Emeka Diallo across the Mosaic April wave and the Sunbelt, Palmetto, and Axiom accounts (or similarly named accounts), including whether reassignment is possible or coverage remains tight.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The response covers Emeka's 5 active accounts including Mosaic Robotics, Sunbelt Venture Partners, Palmetto Community Foundation (Axiom is named as Tideway), and provides a full coordinator workload table concluding no clean reassignment is available.
✓Run #2The response details all coordinator workloads (Suki 9, Javier 8, Fatimah 6, Blessing 5), states no clean reassignment is available, and lists Emeka's accounts including Mosaic, Sunbelt, and Palmetto. Axiom is not mentioned but Tideway replaces it; the criterion allows 'similarly named accounts' and the overall capacity analysis is substantive.
✓Run #3The response provides a coordinator workload table with active move counts for Javier (8), Fatimah (6+2 VIP), and Blessing (5-6), concludes no clean reassignment is available, and notes Emeka's Mosaic/GreenStack load cannot be shifted without risk. Sunbelt, Palmetto, and Axiom are mentioned as accounts in the calendar event description but not explicitly in the final response table — however the overall capacity conclusion is clearly stated.
✓Run #4The response provides a detailed workload table covering Fatimah, Blessing, Javier, and Mina, with assessments of availability. It covers Mosaic, Sunbelt, Palmetto, and Axiom explicitly. The assessment concludes no clean single reassignment is available right now.
✓Run #5The final response provides a coordinator workload table showing all four coordinators at or near capacity and concludes 'No clean reassignment is available.' Mosaic is mentioned; Sunbelt, Palmetto, and Axiom appear in the calendar event title and description in the trajectory. The summary covers coverage tightness and outcome clearly.
✓Run #6The final response summarizes coordinator workloads for Blessing, Javier, and Fatimah, concludes 'No clean full-account reassignment is available,' and explicitly addresses Mosaic (April wave). The calendar event description mentions Sunbelt, Palmetto, and Axiom. The response adequately addresses reassignment feasibility.
3 The final response summarizes the status of Dr. Anand Mehta's executive relocation for Canopy Health regarding housing options and equipment chain-of-custody documentation (or similar wording).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The response summarizes housing (Ticket #343, UrbanNest deadline April 23 passed, no booking confirmation visible) and equipment chain-of-custody (no dedicated ticket exists, unresolved) for Dr. Mehta's Canopy Health relocation.
✓Run #2The response covers both Linear tickets #343 (Temporary Housing, UrbanNest, River North/Streeterville, due May 1) and #396 (Household Move, pack April 30), and explicitly notes no equipment chain-of-custody ticket exists with no dedicated tracking for the medical research equipment.
✓Run #3The response summarizes both workstreams: Ticket #343 (Temporary Housing via UrbanNest/Carmen Reyes, April 23 deadline missed) and Ticket #396 (Household Move, April 30 pack date, equipment chain-of-custody question unresolved). Both housing and equipment/chain-of-custody status are clearly addressed.
✓Run #4The response addresses both housing (#343 — UrbanNest options not sourced, 6 days from move-in) and equipment chain-of-custody (#375 — no evidence chain-of-custody template obtained or specialty crating arranged). Both workstreams are clearly summarized with gap analysis.
✓Run #5The response addresses both housing (Linear #343, UrbanNest sourcing in Streeterville/River North, no options presented to Dr. Mehta yet) and equipment chain-of-custody (two $12K diagnostic monitors, 350-lb filing cabinet, no dedicated Linear ticket found). Both workstreams are covered with specifics.
✓Run #6The final response details housing (#343, UrbanNest options 3 days overdue) and equipment chain-of-custody (#375, monitors and filing cabinet) for Dr. Mehta's Canopy Health relocation, with specific open questions and status for both workstreams.
4 Either the final response states that Swift or Greg Pallone has already provided, committed, or is processing signed placarding documentation for the Keiko Tanaka Mosaic shipment with a clear next step, OR an email was sent (via send_email or reply_to_email) from [chloe.vance@moveops.com](mailto:chloe.vance@moveops.com) to [greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com](mailto:greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) (or reply_to_email in the active Chloe-Greg thread) about the signed DOT placarding certificate or follow-up for that shipment.
outcome

✓Run #6The trajectory shows a conversations_add_message call to #operations tagging @Fatimah with detailed requests for status on all three Mehta workstreams (housing #343, equipment #375, household move #396).

outcome

7 If an email was sent (via send_email or reply_to_email) to [greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com](mailto:greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) about the placarding gap, the subject or body mentions the signed DOT placarding certificate (or placarding or DOT documentation), Keiko Tanaka or Mosaic Robotics, and regulatory, audit, or penalty urgency (or similar).

20 The final response separately addresses whether the signed placarding PDF is in MoveOps or compliance hands versus only promised, in transit, or committed in email from Swift (or states clearly that both matter for the audit packet) (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response explicitly distinguishes file-in-hand from promised: 'His email had no attachments, and no follow-up email with the actual certs exists anywhere in our inbox' vs. 'Greg Pallone replied on ~April 22 confirming... promising scanned PDFs within 24 hours.' This clearly separates promised/committed from received.
✓Run #2The response explicitly distinguishes between what was promised vs. what is in hand: 'Greg's reply email contained no attachments' and 'the actual signed PDF documents have NOT been received' vs. 'He promised scanned PDFs within 24 hours.' The bottom line states 'The cert exists at Swift — but we don't have it in hand.' This clearly separates file-in-hand from carrier commitment.
✓Run #3The response explicitly distinguishes between what was promised vs. what is actually in hand: 'his reply email contained zero attachments' and 'NO documents have been received.' The CRM note also states the distinction between Greg's verbal confirmation/promise and the actual absence of received PDFs. The gap between promised documentation and file-in-hand is clearly articulated.
✓Run #4The response explicitly distinguishes between Greg's commitment ('his reply email on April 22 contained no attachments') and actual documents received ('the actual signed documents have NOT arrived... no follow-up email with PDFs was found anywhere in our inbox'). This clearly separates promised/committed documents from physically received documentation.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly states: 'The certificate is confirmed to exist in Swift's archive but is NOT yet in MoveOps' hands.' It distinguishes between Greg's April 22 promise of scanned PDFs versus actual receipt, making clear the file is promised/in transit but not in MoveOps possession.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly distinguishes between the PDF being promised vs. in-hand: 'His email contained no attachments — only a promise' and 'the actual PDF documents have NOT arrived.' This clearly separates verbal commitment from file-in-hand status.
Total 19/2018/2019/2016/2019/2019/20 92%
